title: Missing measurement at end of circuit
description: A QuantumCircuit is built and executed but no .measure() or .measure_all() call is present. Qiskit simulators return an empty counts dictionary in this case, which is almost always unintentional.
code_snippet:
# Buggy: no measurement
qc = QuantumCircuit(2, 2)
qc.h(0)
qc.cx(0, 1)
# Missing: qc.measure([0, 1], [0, 1])
job = execute(qc, backend)

title: Swapped control and target qubits in CX gate
description: The CX (CNOT) gate expects (control, target). Swapping them produces a logically different circuit that yields incorrect measurement statistics.
code_snippet:
# Buggy: control and target swapped
qc.cx(1, 0)  # intended cx(0, 1)

title: QuantumCircuit initialised with incorrect qubit count
description: Passing the wrong number of qubits to QuantumCircuit() means subsequent gate applications may reference out-of-range qubit indices, raising a CircuitError at runtime.
code_snippet:
# Buggy: only 1 qubit but code applies 2-qubit gate
qc = QuantumCircuit(1)
qc.cx(0, 1)  # IndexError: qubit index 1 out of range

title: Use of deprecated single-qubit gate names (u1/u2/u3)
description: Qiskit deprecated u1, u2, u3 in favour of p, u, and u respectively. Code using the old names may fail or produce warnings on newer Qiskit versions.
code_snippet:
# Deprecated
qc.u1(theta, 0)   # use qc.p(theta, 0)
qc.u2(phi, lam, 0) # use qc.u(pi/2, phi, lam, 0)
qc.u3(theta, phi, lam, 0) # use qc.u(theta, phi, lam, 0)

title: Barrier placed before any gates are added
description: Placing a barrier on an empty circuit or before any gates have been applied serves no purpose and sometimes indicates a structural copy-paste error.
code_snippet:
qc = QuantumCircuit(3)
qc.barrier()  # no gates yet – probably a mistake

title: Classical register size smaller than number of measured qubits
description: If the ClassicalRegister has fewer bits than the number of qubits being measured, Qiskit raises a CircuitError. This often results from off-by-one errors or copy-pasting from a smaller circuit.
code_snippet:
# Buggy: 3 qubits, only 2 classical bits
qr = QuantumRegister(3)
cr = ClassicalRegister(2)
qc = QuantumCircuit(qr, cr)
qc.measure(qr, cr)  # CircuitError: register size mismatch

title: Accessing undefined circuit attribute after transpilation
description: After transpile(), the circuit layout may differ from the original. Accessing qubits by original index without accounting for the new layout leads to incorrect results or KeyErrors.
code_snippet:
from qiskit.compiler import transpile
transipiled = transpile(qc, backend)
# Buggy: assumes qubit 0 is still qubit 0 after transpilation
print(transpiled.qubits[0])

title: Incorrect Toffoli (CCX) qubit argument order
description: The Toffoli gate ccx(control1, control2, target). Misordering the three arguments produces a different unitary and wrong results.
code_snippet:
# Buggy: target supplied before second control
qc.ccx(0, 2, 1)  # intended ccx(0, 1, 2)
